After additional discussion, the Petition for Authority to List and Sell Settlors Commercial Rental Real Property is taken under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Feb-23-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-13-296851 Conservatorship of Karen Rush and
PTR-15-298847 The Karen Rush Revocable Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with David Levin (Conservator/Trustee) by zoom.

The Court questions the request to waive future accountings in the conservatorship, stated that there is a benefit to the Court to get a more complete picture by having an accounting in both the conservatorship and the trust matter, and noted that the requested amount of fees comes out to a pretty sizeable hourly rate. Mr. Schultz stated that the gain of not having an accounting is the expense to the conservatorship and the resources to the Court, that the conservatorship falls under Probate Code 2628 and that the only source of income in the conservatorship is the social security benefit of $400.00 a month. Mr. Schultz also stated that he does not know what the accountings would require from the staff, that gathering materials, reviewing the materials and preparing a petition would at least incur $1,000.00 in Attorney time and his Client's time, and that the conservator's fees are waived for the estate. In addition, Mr. Schultz spoke about Mr. Levin's level of experience as a Professional Fiduciary, stated that administration of the trust is more efficient because of Mr. Levin's level of expertise, that Mr. Levin doesn't have to call him as often as other Fiduciaries do, and that Mr. Levin had to deal with the issue of a commercial tenant. Furthermore, Mr. Schultz spoke of Mr. Levin's costs of doing business, stated that Mr. Levin is self-insured, that Mr. Levin's predecessor got sued while in retirement by a relentless family member, raised the issue of Mr. Levin sometimes not getting paid for his services, stated that the commercial tenant had to close due to COVID which was another issue that Mr. Levin had to deal with and that Mr. Levin voluntarily reduced his fees. The First Account and Report of Successor Conservator and Petition for Its Settlement, etc., and the Amended Fourth Account and Report of Trustee and Petition for Its Settlement, etc. are under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-24-2020 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Continued To Jan-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Andrew Schultz for and with David Levin (Trustee).

The Court stated that the time sheets have fees in the amount of $7,500.00, that the request for fees was for over $13,000.00, and that the time sheets are singularly not helpful in using exactly the same phrase/entry ("for legal correspondence") without any explanation over and over and over again. Mr. Levin stated that it may have to do with their billing software, that it provides canned phrases and it is probably the closest description, that he puts in notes and that he could probably provide more information as to the work that was done for those time entries. Mr. Levin also stated that he is no way duplicating Counsel's time. Mr. Schultz stated that they could submit a further declaration with more detail, that he submitted a supplemental declaration and that there were a lot of complications with the Co-Owner regarding the sale of the property. The Court questions what is meant by the comment of "the difference between the guideline amount and the hourly fee amount" and why it was stated. Mr. Schultz explained that it was done in an effort to find some middle ground and that he believes his Client's level of experience added significant value and a significant savings to the estate. Mr. Levin noted that the hourly fee for Fiduciaries is low in San Francisco. The Third Account and Report of Co-Trustees, etc. is continued to January 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 and will be deemed under submission on that date. The Court is expecting to receive a further declaration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Lelu. Not reported.
